hero.title: Innovation Lab
hero.subtitle: Universidad Galileo · GT
hero.description: A space for experimentation and technological learning where we encourage creativity, teamwork, and building functional prototypes.
hero.footnote: We operate as an open lab: curiosity is the methodology and the prototype is the deliverable.

whatWeDo.title: Research lines of the lab.
whatWeDo.description: We work across six complementary areas. Each combines applied research, rapid prototyping, and collaboration with graduate students.

agentic.title: Agentic & autonomous AI
agentic.description: Systems that reason, decide, and act. Code auditing, decision automation, and agents connected to real tooling.

multimodal.title: Multimodal AI
multimodal.description: Gemini experiments across image, video, audio, and text. Creative generation and media transformation.

robotics.title: Robotics & embodied AI
robotics.description: Arms, quadruped dogs, and robotic hands that react to their surroundings. Perception, navigation, and physical control.

iot.title: Hardware & IoT
iot.description: Prototypes with ESP32, Arduino, and Raspberry Pi. Sensors, actuators, and cloud-connected devices.

creative.title: Creative tools
creative.description: Physical-digital games, generative tools, and experiments that mix hardware, software, and human interaction.

vision.title: Computer vision
vision.description: Real-time detection, tracking, and image analysis. Integration with vehicles, objects, and spaces.

why.title: Learning by doing, with permission to fail.
why.p1: In a world where technology transforms every aspect of our lives, universities need spaces where students can go beyond theory. The Innovation Lab is that space: a place where ideas become functional prototypes and learning happens through practice.
why.p2: We operate with an iterative prototyping and validation methodology, complementing engineering programs with hands-on experience in emerging technologies. Here students don't just learn to code — they learn to innovate, to work as a team, and to solve real problems.
why.p3: Guatemala has enormous technological potential. Through workshops, talks, and applied projects, we aim to boost the innovation and tech entrepreneurship ecosystem in the country, connecting the university with industry and the community.
why.attribution: — Universidad Galileo · Graduate Program in Software Development

social.title: Let's talk.
social.description: Follow us for workshops, demos, and prototypes in development. If you have an idea or want to collaborate, reach out.
social.ctaDescription: An idea, a collab, a question?
